target.name: cylib
target.founder: Lilian Schwich, Gideon Schwich, Paul Sabarny

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $110M - $200M.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: Graphyte
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $200M

private.name: Fourth Power
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $150M

private.name: Steady Energy
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $110M

data.narrative:
Cylib is a Series A climate and energy company founded by Lilian Schwich, Gideon Schwich, and Paul Sabarny. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of 9 private companies and 1 public company (LNZA) in the Climate & Energy sector, with primary focus on 3 same-stage Series A peers: Graphyte ($200M), Fourth Power ($150M), and Steady Energy ($110M).

The peer-median method is the applicable triangulation approach given data availability. The 3 Series A comparables yield a median valuation of $150M, with a range spanning $110M to $200M. This method directly reflects the valuation multiples and investor sentiment for companies at cylib's stage and sector. Cylib has raised €140M+ to date, comprising a €55M Series A alongside €26.1M from NRW and €63.4M from BMWE grants, positioning it as a capital-intensive frontier technology venture.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $128M–$172M, centered on $150M. The median reflects comparable company valuations; the low and high bounds provide confidence intervals around peer dispersion. Key uncertainties include the materiality of non-dilutive grant funding (€89.5M of €140M+) to post-money valuation, relative technology differentiation versus the peer set, and commercialization progress since the Series A close. Limited single-method triangulation constrains precision; revenue multiples or DCF inputs would strengthen the analysis.
